_note: First .factcheck.json for v0.1.9 NCL Prima Western Caribbean Solo Group pack. Built fresh 2026-06-11 during a careful-not-clever pass + content boardroom. Primary-source verification: Wikipedia (Norwegian Prima), NCL press releases (Great Stirrup Cay pier + Great Tides Waterpark Sept 4 2026 opening), CNN + Attractions Magazine + The Points Guy for Prima's 'world's first' claims, Cruise Industry News + multiple sources for the Reykjavik christening claim, Cayman Islands Wikipedia + remitly.com for the KYD peg correction, jm.usembassy.gov for US Consular Agency George Town.
verification_session: Sailing is Sept 27 - Oct 4, 2026 (108 days out). Full read of all 817 pre-fix lines, primary-source verification of every suspect claim, factual corrections, voice pass, content boardroom (triad x 2 vendors blind), adopted-content execution.

length_ft_m.value: 965 ft / 294 m per NCL's published spec (the christening press release uses 965 ft); Wikipedia article cites 299 m / 981 ft. The two figures differ by ~16 ft and likely reflect different conventions (LOA vs waterline, or rounding); pack uses NCL's marketing figure with attribution.

capacity_double_max.value: 3,099 guests at double occupancy per Wikipedia; max capacity not asserted in pack body after correction (the prior '~3,950 max' was not sourced to a primary).

staterooms.value: 1,646 cabins per pack body; not in Wikipedia.
staterooms.source: Pack-cited; flag for primary-source check before any wider distribution.

crew.notes: CORRECTED from pack's '~1,388' — the same hedged-wrong number that was wrong on the Aqua and Luna packs earlier in this batch. Third occurrence of the same training-data carryover.

year_built.value: 2022 — launched 13 August 2021; delivered 29 July 2022; christened 27 August 2022.
year_built.notes: Pack body delivery date precision improved from generic 'August 2022' to 'delivered 29 July 2022.'

class.value: Prima Class — LEAD SHIP. Sisters: Norwegian Viva (2023, original Prima Class); Norwegian Aqua (2025, Prima Plus subclass); Norwegian Luna (2026, Prima Plus).

godparent.value: Katy Perry (pop singer; performed at the Reykjavik christening)

date.value: 27 August 2022 in Reykjavik, Iceland — verified as the first major cruise ship christening ever held in the Icelandic capital
date.source: Multiple primary sources including Cruise Industry News + Travel News Asia + Travel Agent Central

auto_gratuity_rate.value: $20/person/day standard cabins; $25/person/day Suites and Haven (2026 rates).

ship_phone_relay.value: 1-888-627-4477 — NCL's paid ship-phone relay (credit card required, per-minute rate). State Dept OCS line at 1-888-407-4747 / 1-202-501-4444 (24/7, free) is the right path for actual emergencies.
ship_phone_relay.notes: CORRECTION: pack previously carried '+1 (305) 436-4000 / +1 (866) 234-0292' as 'NCL Care Team' — same unverified pair that was on Bliss/Luna pre-fix. Handoff card + 'what an at-home contact does' both updated.

great_stirrup_cay_pier_status.claim: Prima docks at the new multi-ship pier completed late 2025 — no tendering. The pier was closed April through summer 2026 for further construction (tenders ran in that window); by the Sept 29 sailing it has reopened, currently accommodating one large ship alongside.
great_stirrup_cay_pier_status.source: https://travelhost.com/cruises/norwegian-cruise-line-opens-private-island-pier + https://www.norwegiancruiselineblog.com/blog/great-stirrup-cay-destination-guide (confirmed: pier open, second-ship capability still finishing)
great_stirrup_cay_pier_status.disposition: CORRECTION — pack previously claimed tender port; for this specific sailing the pier is operational.

great_tides_waterpark_opening.claim: Great Tides Waterpark opened September 4, 2026 — 25 days before this Sept 29 sailing.
great_tides_waterpark_opening.disposition: CORRECTION — pack previously hedged 'scheduled to open September 2026, may be soft-opening or pre-launch.' For this sailing it's fully open.

great_life_lagoon_size.claim: NCL has published BOTH figures: '1.4-acre pool area' (announcement release 721) and '28,000 square-foot pool' (winter-opening release 756). The 28,000 figure appears to be the water surface; the 1.4-acre figure the whole zone including decking, cabanas, splash pad. Both are NCL-attested.
great_life_lagoon_size.disposition: Pack body now cites both with attribution. This resolves the earlier batch confusion (Luna sidecar flagged the 28k figure as wrong; turns out both NCL sources use different framings).

great_stirrup_cay_size.notes: Pack body previously said '~250 acres'; NCL's own number is 270 acres. Corrected this pass.

splash_cay_name.claim: 'Splash Cay' is the published name as of NCL's Sept 4 2026 grand-opening release.
splash_cay_name.notes: Pack previously hedged 'NCL's map labels it Splash Harbor — naming still settling.' Hedge removed; NCL has settled on Splash Cay.

the_drop_worlds_first.claim: The Drop — world's first freefall dry slide at sea.

speedway_worlds_first.claim: Prima Speedway — world's first three-level go-kart track at sea (1,378 ft, 14 sharp turns, 15 racers, top speed 30 mph). NCL's earlier Norwegian Joy 2017 introduced go-karts at sea; Prima introduced the three-level configuration.
speedway_worlds_first.disposition: VERIFIED — claim retained with the existing pack-body nuance about Joy as the first go-kart-at-sea ship.

cayman_currency_peg.claim: Cayman Islands dollar (KYD) pegged at 1 KYD = 1.20 USD since 1974. The Cayman dollar is STRONGER than the U.S. dollar. Tourist practical rate: $1 USD = ~$0.80 KYD when receiving change.
cayman_currency_peg.disposition: MAJOR CORRECTION — pack had the peg BACKWARDS at TWO locations: '1.20 KYD = 1 USD' would mean KYD is weaker, which is the opposite of reality. Real-money error for a buyer who paid in USD assuming change-back math. Both locations corrected.

us_consular_agency_grand_cayman.claim: U.S. Consular Agency George Town, Cayman Islands: +1-345-747-8172 (Mon-Fri 8 AM - 2 PM). After-hours: U.S. Embassy Kingston Jamaica +1-876-702-6000.
us_consular_agency_grand_cayman.disposition: CORRECTION — handoff card previously routed Grand Cayman emergencies to US Embassy Kingston without naming the actual Cayman consular agency. Now lists both with after-hours routing.

first_major_cruise_ship_christened_reykj.claim: First major cruise ship christened in Reykjavík, Iceland

ncls_oldest_private_destination.claim: Great Stirrup Cay — NCL has owned and operated since 1977 — the oldest cruise-line private destination in the Caribbean.
ncls_oldest_private_destination.source: Industry coverage of cruise-line private islands; consistent across sources.

templated_section_headers.notes: 'Honest read on [port]' templated headers on Sitka equivalent / Falmouth / Grand Cayman / Cozumel / Great Stirrup Cay + 'The honest read on hurricane risk' all varied to port-specific or topic-specific framings. Same fix pattern as the six prior batch packs (Symphony / Virgin / Aqua / Seaside / Luna / Bliss + Anthem retrofit).

iconic_prose_frequency.notes: Reduced via L351 'iconic experience' -> 'trip-defining experience'; remaining two ('iconic Cozumel experience' L376 and 'iconic stretch of west-coast white sand' in the original Grand Cayman para were combined into the corrected Cayman paragraph and the Cozumel inline). Within v2.3.0 doctrine.

first_person_attestation_with_date.verdict: MISSING in the editor's voice — but Tina's lived authority is quoted as hers (her host's standard rhythm, her sailaway gathering pattern, her standing 5:30 Hudson's). The Welcome's new attestation note names the boundary explicitly.

honest_limitation_acknowledged.verdict: present — hurricane-season insurance framing, Great Stirrup pier contingency, source-conflict on Prima length, NCL refunds vs trip-insurance lines, 'if Tina's communication goes quiet' guidance, both Welcome attestation paragraphs

from_the_pier_specificity.verdict: strong — Port Canaveral garage $17-22 vs PortMiami $35, the multi-ship pier status timeline, the practical KYD/USD change-back math, Stingray City independent $60 vs ship $80-130, Palancar independent $40-60 vs ship $80-110, Cozumel pier specifics (Punta Langosta / International / Puerta Maya)

signals: brand triplet ('no urgency, no upsell, no countdown timer'), single instance
Tina's host attribution preserved separately from research voice
specific Sept 4 2026 Great Tides opening verified post-cutoff
Cayman peg correction (the kind of error that proves a human checker actually checked)
hurricane-window playbook section content is recognizably operational not aspirational
Welcome attestation note explicitly names what's first-hand vs researched
Studio cabin 'no single supplement' framed as a real economic mechanism not a marketing line
the 1 KYD = 1.20 USD practical change-back math is operational specificity
US Consular Agency George Town hours block
NCL refunds vs travel insurance line distinction is specific not generic
the 'if Tina's communication goes quiet' fallback to bridge channel is real procedural detail

cluster_detection.verdict_justification: Zero Layer 1 signals; Layer 2 within v2.3.0 allowances; 12 Layer 3 counter-signals. The pack carries the strongest hurricane-window operational specificity of any pack in the catalog because the sailing is in peak hurricane window; that operational depth IS the trust signal.

voice_audit.promotional_drift_check: pass — no CTAs, no urgency framings, no upsell language. Sec 7 hurricane content is risk-honest, not fear-driven.
voice_audit.risk_rating_justification: All voice-tell counts at or below batch thresholds; major factual landmines (KYD peg backwards, NCL Care Team unverified numbers, tender-vs-pier status, Great Tides opening date, crew count carryover) all corrected against primary sources; boardroom-adopted hurricane-playbook and group-dynamics content is operational and corpus-native.
recommended_followup_edits: If Tina publishes a specific hurricane-contingency plan for this group before sailing, replace the generic playbook in Sec 7 with her version.
1,646 staterooms claim not in Wikipedia — verify against NCL deck plan PDF.
Length discrepancy: NCL press release 965 ft vs Wikipedia 299 m (981 ft) — investigate which convention NCL's own deck plan uses.
Operator hand-edit pass planned per operator's standing note (the pack should read as substantively the operator's).
